description: Gate the .devcontainer boot path in CI: add a boot-lint job (shellcheck + hadolint) and extend ci-harness.yml path filters so a broken entrypoint.sh, Dockerfile, or install script can no longer merge green.

userStories.title: Extend CI path filters to cover the boot path
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want CI to trigger on .devcontainer/ and install/ changes so a broken boot path can never merge with a green badge.
acceptanceCriteria: ci-harness.yml push.paths block includes ".devcontainer/**" and "install/**"
ci-harness.yml pull_request.paths block includes ".devcontainer/**" and "install/**"
grep -c '\.devcontainer/\*\*' .github/workflows/ci-harness.yml returns 2 (one per trigger block)
Dockerfile is covered transitively (it lives at .devcontainer/Dockerfile)
YAML still parses without error

userStories.title: Add a curated .hadolint.yaml ignore list
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want hadolint's accepted dev-sandbox warnings documented in one place so the Dockerfile gate is green-by-design yet still fails on new, un-accepted findings.
acceptanceCriteria: A new file .hadolint.yaml exists at the repo root
It contains an ignored: list with exactly: DL4006, DL3008, DL3016, DL3003, DL3059, SC2016
Each ignored rule carries a brief inline comment explaining the accepted tradeoff
A header comment notes the validated hadolint version (hadolint-action v3.1.0 bundled binary; rule families stable across hadolint 2.x)
hadolint --config .hadolint.yaml .devcontainer/Dockerfile exits 0 with no output
File parses as valid YAML

userStories.title: Add the boot-lint CI job
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a dedicated job that runs shellcheck and hadolint on the boot path so defects are caught in CI, not at sandbox rebuild time.
acceptanceCriteria: A new boot-lint job is added to the jobs: section of ci-harness.yml
The job runs on ubuntu-latest and checks out via actions/checkout@v4
The job runs: command -v shellcheck >/dev/null || sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y shellcheck
The job runs: shellcheck -S warning .devcontainer/*.sh install/*.sh
The job runs hadolint against .devcontainer/Dockerfile with config: .hadolint.yaml via hadolint/hadolint-action SHA-pinned @54c9adbab1582c2ef04b2016b760714a4bfde3cf # v3.1.0
The job has NO needs: field (runs independently of and in parallel with the ci job)
The job does not override the workflow-level permissions: contents: read or the ci-harness-${{ github.ref }} concurrency group in a way that weakens either
YAML still parses without error

userStories.title: Record the change in the CHANGELOG
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the new gate noted in the changelog so the release notes capture it.
acceptanceCriteria: One bullet added under ## [Unreleased] -> ### Added in CHANGELOG.md
The entry is one line, imperative mood, and references ([#26](https://github.com/ryaneggz/openharness/issues/26))
No versioned section is hand-edited
